instruction: How do I set up a TypeScript monorepo with Turborepo, shared types, and workspace protocols?
output:
<think>
Turborepo is a build system for JavaScript/TypeScript monorepos. It caches build outputs and runs tasks in parallel based on the dependency graph between packages. The key concepts: workspaces (npm/pnpm/yarn), pipeline configuration in turbo.json, and remote caching.

The shared types pattern is critical: create a `packages/types` workspace that exports TypeScript interfaces and types. Other packages depend on it. When types change, Turborepo knows which downstream packages need rebuilding.

Pnpm workspaces are preferred over npm/yarn for monorepos because pnpm uses hard links (saves disk space), has strict dependency isolation (no phantom dependencies), and workspace protocol (`workspace:*`) ensures you always use the local version of workspace packages.

The pipeline in turbo.json defines task dependencies: `build` depends on `^build` (build dependencies first), `test` depends on `build` (test after build). Turborepo topologically sorts and parallelizes.
</think>

```typescript
// === Project structure ===
// monorepo/
// +-- turbo.json
// +-- pnpm-workspace.yaml
// +-- package.json (root)
// +-- packages/
// |   +-- types/          (shared TypeScript types)
// |   |   +-- package.json
// |   |   +-- src/index.ts
// |   +-- ui/             (shared React components)
// |   |   +-- package.json
// |   |   +-- src/Button.tsx
// +-- apps/
//     +-- web/            (Next.js app)
//     |   +-- package.json
//     +-- api/            (Express API)
//         +-- package.json

// === pnpm-workspace.yaml ===
// packages:
//   - "packages/*"
//   - "apps/*"

// === turbo.json ===
// {
//   "pipeline": {
//     "build": {
//       "dependsOn": ["^build"],
//       "outputs": ["dist/**"]
//     },
//     "test": {
//       "dependsOn": ["build"]
//     },
//     "lint": {},
//     "dev": {
//       "cache": false,
//       "persistent": true
//     }
//   }
// }

// === packages/types/src/index.ts ===
export interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  role: 'admin' | 'user';
}

export interface ApiResponse<T> {
  data: T;
  error?: string;
  timestamp: number;
}

export interface PaginatedResponse<T> extends ApiResponse<T[]> {
  total: number;
  page: number;
  pageSize: number;
}

// === packages/types/package.json ===
// {
//   "name": "@monorepo/types",
//   "version": "0.0.0",
//   "main": "./dist/index.js",
//   "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
//   "scripts": { "build": "tsc" }
// }

// === apps/web/package.json (using workspace protocol) ===
// {
//   "name": "@monorepo/web",
//   "dependencies": {
//     "@monorepo/types": "workspace:*",
//     "@monorepo/ui": "workspace:*"
//   }
// }

// === apps/api/src/routes.ts (imports shared types) ===
import type { User, ApiResponse } from '@monorepo/types';

function getUser(id: string): ApiResponse<User> {
  return {
    data: { id, name: 'Alice', email: 'a@b.com', role: 'admin' },
    timestamp: Date.now(),
  };
}

// Commands:
// pnpm install            # install all workspace deps
// pnpm turbo build        # build all packages in dependency order
// pnpm turbo test         # test all (after building)
// pnpm turbo dev --filter=@monorepo/web  # dev server for web only
```

instruction: How do I share TypeScript configuration across a monorepo so all packages have consistent settings?
output:
<think>
In a TypeScript monorepo, you want consistent compiler settings across all packages but with the flexibility to override per-package. The pattern is a base tsconfig in the root or a shared config package, with per-package tsconfigs that extend it.

Create `packages/tsconfig/` with preset configs: base.json (shared settings), react.json (extends base + JSX), node.json (extends base + Node types). Each app/package extends the appropriate preset.

The key settings to share: strict mode, target, module resolution, path aliases. Settings that vary per package: outDir, rootDir, include/exclude, references.

Project references (composite: true + references in tsconfig) enable incremental builds across the monorepo. TypeScript only rebuilds packages whose sources changed. Combined with Turborepo caching, this makes large monorepo builds fast.
</think>

```typescript
// === packages/tsconfig/base.json ===
// {
//   "compilerOptions": {
//     "strict": true,
//     "esModuleInterop": true,
//     "skipLibCheck": true,
//     "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
//     "resolveJsonModule": true,
//     "isolatedModules": true,
//     "declaration": true,
//     "declarationMap": true,
//     "sourceMap": true
//   }
// }

// === packages/tsconfig/node.json ===
// {
//   "extends": "./base.json",
//   "compilerOptions": {
//     "module": "commonjs",
//     "target": "ES2022",
//     "moduleResolution": "node",
//     "outDir": "./dist",
//     "rootDir": "./src"
//   }
// }

// === packages/tsconfig/react.json ===
// {
//   "extends": "./base.json",
//   "compilerOptions": {
//     "module": "ESNext",
//     "target": "ES2022",
//     "moduleResolution": "bundler",
//     "jsx": "react-jsx",
//     "lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"]
//   }
// }

// === apps/api/tsconfig.json ===
// {
//   "extends": "@monorepo/tsconfig/node.json",
//   "compilerOptions": {
//     "outDir": "./dist",
//     "rootDir": "./src"
//   },
//   "include": ["src/**/*"],
//   "references": [
//     { "path": "../../packages/types" }
//   ]
// }

// === apps/web/tsconfig.json ===
// {
//   "extends": "@monorepo/tsconfig/react.json",
//   "compilerOptions": {
//     "baseUrl": ".",
//     "paths": {
//       "@/*": ["./src/*"]
//     }
//   },
//   "include": ["src/**/*", "next-env.d.ts"],
//   "references": [
//     { "path": "../../packages/types" },
//     { "path": "../../packages/ui" }
//   ]
// }

// With project references, build incrementally:
// tsc --build             # builds only changed packages
// tsc --build --watch     # incremental watch mode

// Consistent across all packages:
// - strict: true everywhere
// - Same ES target
// - Same module resolution strategy
// - Shared path aliases pattern
```

instruction: How do I configure path aliases in a TypeScript monorepo so imports work in both development and production?
output:
<think>
Path aliases let you write `import { User } from '@/models/user'` instead of `import { User } from '../../../models/user'`. The challenge in monorepos: aliases must work in TypeScript compilation, bundler resolution, and test runner resolution simultaneously.

TypeScript handles aliases via `paths` in tsconfig.json. But TypeScript only uses paths for type checking, not for emitted JavaScript. The bundler (webpack, Vite, Next.js) needs its own alias config. And Jest/Vitest need `moduleNameMapper`.

The cleanest solution: use the bundler's native alias support and configure TypeScript to match. For Next.js, the `@/*` alias works out of the box with tsconfig paths. For Vite, use `resolve.alias`. For Jest, use `moduleNameMapper`.

For workspace package imports, pnpm's `workspace:*` protocol handles resolution automatically. No path aliases needed for cross-package imports.
</think>

```typescript
// === tsconfig.json (TypeScript type checking) ===
// {
//   "compilerOptions": {
//     "baseUrl": ".",
//     "paths": {
//       "@/*": ["./src/*"],
//       "@components/*": ["./src/components/*"],
//       "@utils/*": ["./src/utils/*"],
//       "@hooks/*": ["./src/hooks/*"]
//     }
//   }
// }

// === vite.config.ts (Vite bundler) ===
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import path from 'path';

export default defineConfig({
  resolve: {
    alias: {
      '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
      '@components': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/components'),
      '@utils': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/utils'),
      '@hooks': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/hooks'),
    },
  },
});

// === vitest.config.ts (test runner) ===
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';
import path from 'path';

export default defineConfig({
  test: {
    globals: true,
  },
  resolve: {
    alias: {
      '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
    },
  },
});

// === Usage — works in dev, build, and tests ===
import { formatDate } from '@utils/date';
import { Button } from '@components/Button';
import { useAuth } from '@hooks/useAuth';

// For cross-package imports in monorepo (no aliases needed):
import type { User } from '@monorepo/types';  // workspace:* handles this

// Common mistake: path aliases in tsconfig but not in bundler
// TypeScript compiles fine but runtime fails because JS has unresolved paths
// Always configure BOTH tsconfig AND bundler/test runner
```

instruction: How do I set up shared ESLint and Prettier configuration across a TypeScript monorepo?
output:
<think>
Shared linting config in a monorepo ensures consistent code style across all packages. The pattern: create a `packages/eslint-config` package with preset configs, then each app/package extends it.

With ESLint flat config (eslint.config.js, the new default), you export arrays of config objects from the shared package and spread them in each project. This is cleaner than the old .eslintrc extends pattern.

For Prettier, create a shared config package that exports the Prettier options. Each package references it in package.json with `prettier: @monorepo/prettier-config`.

The key is making these configs workspace packages so they are resolved via pnpm's workspace protocol. This means changes to linting rules are immediately reflected across all packages without publishing.

For performance, use Turborepo to cache lint results. If source files have not changed, the lint task is skipped entirely.
</think>

```typescript
// === packages/eslint-config/index.js (flat config) ===
import tsPlugin from '@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin';
import tsParser from '@typescript-eslint/parser';

export const baseConfig = [
  {
    files: ['**/*.ts', '**/*.tsx'],
    languageOptions: {
      parser: tsParser,
      parserOptions: {
        ecmaVersion: 'latest',
        sourceType: 'module',
      },
    },
    plugins: { '@typescript-eslint': tsPlugin },
    rules: {
      '@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars': ['error', { argsIgnorePattern: '^_' }],
      '@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any': 'warn',
      '@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type': 'off',
      'no-console': ['warn', { allow: ['warn', 'error'] }],
    },
  },
];

export const reactConfig = [
  ...baseConfig,
  {
    files: ['**/*.tsx'],
    rules: {
      'react/react-in-jsx-scope': 'off', // not needed with React 17+
    },
  },
];

// === packages/prettier-config/index.json ===
// {
//   "semi": true,
//   "singleQuote": true,
//   "trailingComma": "all",
//   "printWidth": 100,
//   "tabWidth": 2
// }

// === apps/api/eslint.config.js ===
import { baseConfig } from '@monorepo/eslint-config';

export default [
  ...baseConfig,
  {
    rules: {
      'no-console': 'off', // server-side can use console
    },
  },
];

// === apps/web/eslint.config.js ===
import { reactConfig } from '@monorepo/eslint-config';

export default [...reactConfig];

// === apps/api/package.json ===
// {
//   "prettier": "@monorepo/prettier-config",
//   "devDependencies": {
//     "@monorepo/eslint-config": "workspace:*",
//     "@monorepo/prettier-config": "workspace:*"
//   }
// }

// turbo.json: lint is cacheable
// { "pipeline": { "lint": { "outputs": [] } } }
// pnpm turbo lint  # runs lint across all packages, cached
```
